commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Magna Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Magna's Australian-market engines are robust but known for specific failure patterns - particularly the 6G72 V6's cold-start sensor issues and distributor failures, and the 4G54's timing chain tensioner wear. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: 6G72 Coolant Temperature Sensor Failure - Why Magna Engines Won't Start Cold
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Magna TR, TS, TE, TF, TH (1991-2002, 6G72 V6)
problems.rootCause: Original coolant temperature sensor degrades, sending incorrect cold-start fuel mixture data to ECU.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace CTS sensor, reset ECU adaptives
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes cold-start only; won't prevent other 6G72 failures

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace CTS, distributor cap/rotor, plugs, leads
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses multiple 6G72 weak points; suitable if engine otherwise healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G72 with upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Magnas or when bottom-end wear suspected

problems.recommendation: If your Magna only struggles to start when cold and runs fine once warm, start with the sensor replacement. But if you're also experiencing misfires under load or high mileage (100k+), a rebuilt 6G72 with warranty often represents better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G72 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Magna

problems.h4: 6G72 Distributor Rotor Cross-Firing - Ignition Breakup Under Load
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Magna TR, TS, TE, TF (1991-2000, 6G72 V6)
problems.rootCause: Original distributor rotor develops carbon tracking, causing spark to jump to wrong cylinder under acceleration.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace rotor, cap, plugs; clean contacts
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix; may recur if wiring harness brittle

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New distributor assembly, leads, coil, CTS, plugs
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive; suitable if engine compression healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G72 with upgraded ignition components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if misfires caused catalyst damage or engine has 100k+ miles

problems.recommendation: For Magnas under 90k miles with otherwise good service history, the full ignition overhaul is cost-effective. But if the misfire has triggered a check-engine light or catalytic converter codes, replacement becomes more economical.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned 6G72 Mitsubishi Magna engine with warranty

problems.h4: 4G54 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Astron II Engine Rattle
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure; chain slap damages guides and can jump timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, inspect guides, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early before chain jump

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4G54 with upgraded tensioner system
repairOptions.longevity: Often the only safe option once rattling is audible

problems.recommendation: The 4G54 is an interference engine - if you hear chain rattle, stop driving immediately. For first-gen Magnas, replacement is usually more reliable than attempting a tensioner-only repair on a high-mileage unit.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4G54 Mitsubishi Magna engine replacement

problems.h4: 6G74 Valve Train Noise - Hydraulic Lifter Tick on Verada/TJ Engines
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Magna TH (AWD), TJ, Verada KW (1999-2005, 6G74 3.5L)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic lifters wear or oil passages clog, causing persistent ticking at idle.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine flush, premium oil, new filter
repairOptions.longevity: May quiet noise temporarily; not a permanent fix

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove heads, replace lifters, clean oil galleries
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block compression healthy and noise is isolated

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 6G74 with upgraded valvetrain components
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution for high-mileage Verada/TJ engines

problems.recommendation: If the ticking is accompanied by oil consumption or power loss, replacement is usually more economical than head work on a 20+ year old engine. For low-mileage examples with isolated noise, head service may suffice.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G74 Mitsubishi Magna engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Magna
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi Magna's Australian-market engines are robust when maintained, but known failure points can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand grey-import compatibility. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original weaknesses, backed by a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes and get your imported Magna back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Magna Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Magna and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.intro: Every Mitsubishi Magna engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Mitsubishi Magna Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally restored engines with worn parts replaced; balanced performance and value for grey-import Magnas.
types.frontDescription: Professionally restored engines with worn parts replaced; balanced performance and value for grey-import Magnas.
types.frontDisclaimer: Prices vary by engine code, variant and supplier availability.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Magna engines undergo partial disassembly, cleaning, and replacement of worn components like bearings, seals and gaskets. Ideal for TR-TF 6G72 or TM-TP 4G54 engines with moderate wear.
backBullets: Includes upgraded tensioners/sensors addressing known Magna failure points

Typical turnaround 3-5 working days from order to dispatch

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Magna Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Magna's 1985-2005 production timeline, organised so grey-import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original TM Magna introduced Mitsubishi's Australian-market mid-size sedan with the robust 4G54 Astron II inline-four.
keyChanges: Launch of first-generation Magna sedan and wagon (TM chassis)
ECI Multi electronic fuel injection adopted for Australian conditions
knownFor: Simple SOHC design aids rebuild affordability
Timing chain tensioner wear common after 90k miles
Robust block but brittle early wiring harnesses
years.cta: Get quotes for 1985-1987 Mitsubishi Magna replacement engines

years.description: The TN update refined the first-gen Magna with minor styling tweaks and improved ECU mapping for the 4G54 engine.
keyChanges: TN chassis facelift with revised grille and trim
ECU calibration updates for improved cold-start behaviour
Wagon variant gains popularity in Australian fleet market
knownFor: Same 4G54 reliability profile as TM
Slightly improved wiring harness durability
Early examples now rare in UK grey-import market
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1989 Mitsubishi Magna replacement engines

years.description: The TP represented the final evolution of the first-generation Magna before the all-new TR platform arrived.
keyChanges: TP chassis with updated interior and suspension tuning
Final refinement of 4G54 ECI Multi system
Introduction of limited-run sport trim packages
knownFor: Most refined first-gen 4G54 applications
Still susceptible to timing chain tensioner wear
Good candidate for budget rebuild projects in UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 1989-1991 Mitsubishi Magna replacement engines

years.description: The all-new TR/TS platform introduced the 6G72 Cyclone V6, defining the Magna's identity for the next decade.
keyChanges: New TR/TS chassis with improved aerodynamics and interior space
Verada luxury variant launched with same 6G72 engine
knownFor: 6G72 cold-start issues due to coolant sensor degradation
Distributor rotor cross-firing under load (pre-1996 units)
Strong mid-range torque popular with Australian fleet buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 1991-1996 Mitsubishi Magna replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Magna TR engine replacement • 6G72 hard start cold • Magna TS distributor failure

years.description: The TE/TF update refined the second-gen Magna with styling updates and incremental improvements to the 6G72 engine.
keyChanges: TE/TF facelift with revised front end and interior trim
Updated ECU mapping to address cold-start behaviour
Introduction of improved wiring harness materials
knownFor: Improved cold-start reliability over TR/TS
Distributor issues persist but less frequent
Good balance of affordability and parts availability in UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2000 Mitsubishi Magna replacement engines

years.description: The final Magna generation introduced the 3.5L 6G74 V6 for flagship variants and rare AWD applications.
keyChanges: New TH/TJ chassis with modernised styling and safety features
Introduction of 6G74 3.5L V6 for TJ and Verada KW
Limited-run AWD variant (TH/TJ) using 6G74 with modified drivetrain
knownFor: 6G74 valve train noise on high-mileage examples
AWD variant extremely rare in UK grey-import market
Final-year TJ models command premium for rebuild projects

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Magna Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mitsubishi Magna engine in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, if your grey-import Magna is otherwise in good condition. Rebuilt engines cost £1,800-£3,000 versus £4,000+ for a comparable replacement vehicle, making replacement economical for well-maintained imports.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 6G72 engines typically cost £2,000-£2,800 supply-only
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on workshop location
12-month warranty protects your investment against premature failure
Grey-import Magnas retain enthusiast value in UK classic markets
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mitsubishi Magna engine prices for your variant

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Magna engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices range £1,800-£3,000 depending on engine code (4G54, 6G72, 6G74), with supply & fit adding £450-£850 for labour.
keyPoints: 4G54 2.6L engines: £1,800-£2,400 rebuilt supply-only
6G72 3.0L V6 engines: £2,000-£2,800 rebuilt supply-only
6G74 3.5L V6 engines: £2,200-£3,000 rebuilt supply-only
Prices exclude VAT, delivery and any ancillary parts replacement
items.cta: Get a tailored Mitsubishi Magna engine quote today

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Magna 6G72 engines to fail to start when cold?
items.answer: The most common cause is coolant temperature sensor failure, sending incorrect data to the ECU and causing lean cold-start mixture.
keyPoints: Faulty CTS triggers long crank/no-start only when engine is cold
Replacement sensor costs £25-£60 plus 1-2 hours labour
If misfires persist after CTS replacement, check distributor rotor
Rebuilt engines include upgraded sensors addressing this weakness
items.cta: Find a reconditioned 6G72 Mitsubishi Magna engine with warranty

items.question: Will a 6G72 engine from a TR Magna fit my TF Magna?
items.answer: Yes, 6G72 engines are broadly interchangeable across TR-TF generations, but verify ECU compatibility and wiring harness connectors before purchase.
keyPoints: Physical mounting points identical across TR-TF 6G72 applications
ECU pinouts may differ; confirm with supplier before ordering
Wiring harness connectors changed slightly between 1991-2000
AWD TH/TJ models require 6G74-specific engines, not 6G72
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi Magna engine compatibility for your variant

items.question: How long does it take to fit a replacement Mitsubishi Magna engine?
items.answer: Professional supply & fit typically requires 2-3 working days for straightforward 6G72 swaps, longer for AWD or Verada variants due to complexity.
keyPoints: Standard RWD Magna: 2-3 days including ECU reset and road test
AWD or Verada variants: 3-4 days due to additional drivetrain components
Allow extra time for wiring harness adaptation on grey-import applications
Most UK specialists offer collection/delivery to reduce owner downtime
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Magna engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: What is the difference between Mitsubishi Magna and Verada engines?
items.answer: Early Verada KR used the same 6G72 3.0L V6 as Magna TR-TF; later Verada KW used the larger 6G74 3.5L V6 not fitted to standard Magna models.
keyPoints: Verada KR (1992-1996): 6G72 3.0L V6, interchangeable with Magna TR-TS
Verada KW (1999-2003): 6G74 3.5L V6, higher output, different mounting
6G74 engines require specific ECU and wiring harness compatibility checks
Verada luxury trim does not affect engine replacement pricing significantly
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Verada engine prices for KR or KW variants

items.question: Can I replace a Mitsubishi Magna engine myself in the UK?
items.answer: Technically possible for experienced DIYers with proper equipment, but grey-import wiring and AWD complexity make professional fitting strongly recommended.
keyPoints: Requires engine hoist, transmission jack and specialist diagnostic tools
Australian-market wiring may differ from UK-spec Mitsubishi vehicles
ECU adaptation and immobiliser pairing often require dealer-level equipment
Professional fitting preserves warranty coverage on rebuilt engines
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Magna engine supply & fit from UK specialists

items.question: Do Mitsubishi Magna replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists.
keyPoints: Warranty covers internal components against manufacturing defects
Labour for warranty claims typically not included; confirm terms upfront
Keep service records and oil change receipts to maintain warranty validity
Some suppliers offer extended warranty options for additional cost
items.cta: View Mitsubishi Magna engine warranty terms from our network suppliers

items.question: How do I identify which engine code my Mitsubishi Magna has?
items.answer: Check the engine identification plate on the firewall or strut tower, or decode your VIN; common codes are 4G54, 6G72, or 6G74.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near cylinder head on driver's side
VIN decoding: 10th character indicates model year, engine code in positions 4-6
Registration lookup via DVLA may not specify Australian-market engine codes
When in doubt, provide photos of engine bay to supplier for verification
items.cta: Get help identifying your Mitsubishi Magna engine code

items.question: Where can I find a Mitsubishi Magna engine specialist in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists experienced in grey-import Australian vehicles, including Magna and Verada applications.
keyPoints: Not all UK engine rebuilders stock Australian-market engine components
Specialists familiar with 6G72/6G74 wiring and ECU systems reduce fitment risk
Compare multiple quotes to balance price, warranty terms and turnaround time
Phone consultation recommended for complex AWD or Verada applications
